## 1. Executive Summary

Universe Simulation is a multiplayer 3D browser game set inside a procedurally generated planet. Players sign in, spawn into a shared open world, and survive using the same physical laws that govern the simulation itself. Fire is not a scripted effect — it is real Arrhenius combustion chemistry running in a grid of cells. Metal smelting is not an animation — it is a real reduction reaction between ore and carbon at the right temperature. The world has a day and night cycle driven by a rotating sun. Rain and snow fall from a Markov-chain weather system. Everything visible is emergent from simulation rules, not scripted.

As of this report (version 10, 2026-03-21), the game has completed milestones M0 through M12. The full survival loop is playable end-to-end: a player can spawn with nothing, gather stone and wood, craft tools, light a fire using real chemistry, cook food, drink from rivers, treat wounds with herbs, build shelter to sleep in, smelt copper, smelt iron, craft leather armor, navigate a dynamic seasonal world, sail rivers and coasts by wind power, fish for food, trade copper coins with five economically specialized NPC settlements, fire a musket using real gunpowder chemistry, observe real star constellations through a telescope, defend settlements with castle walls and siege mechanics, conduct diplomacy between settlements, and now launch a rocket into space and broadcast radio signals across the world.

Milestone 11 was a four-system parallel delivery covering the late medieval period. Gunpowder chemistry added a real Arrhenius reaction between KNO3, carbon, and sulfur — with saltpeter forming in nitrogen-rich soil near animal waste and cave floors, sulfur found in volcanic zones, and the musket (iron barrel and powder charge) as the first ranged firearm. Castle structures introduced a multi-block wall, tower, and gate system with a CastleRenderer and siege mechanics; city walls unlock at civLevel 4-5. Astronomy added a real star particle system using actual constellation positions, a craftable telescope (4 iron + 2 glass), moon phase tracking with eclipse timing, and a night-sky HUD overlay. Diplomacy introduced mayor NPCs (the highest-trust NPC in a settlement), diplomatic envoys that travel between settlements, and war and peace declarations that affect the player's criminal alignment.

Milestone 12 brought the Space Age. RocketSystem.ts implements a countdown-to-ignition-to-ascent state machine and broadcasts ROCKET_LAUNCHED over WebSocket with screen shake. RocketVFXRenderer.tsx produces a three-layer exhaust cone (white core, orange glow, soot dispersal, 180 particles) with a ShaderMaterial heat shimmer and a scorch disc on the launch pad. RadioSystem.ts manages a broadcast queue and tower registry, sending PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST WebSocket messages when a radio tower is activated. RadioTowerVFXRenderer.tsx draws teal electromagnetic pulse rings using additive blending at one ring per two seconds. ElectricLightPass.tsx places 40 tungsten-filament PointLights (2700K warm white, color #FFBA74) over civLevel 6 settlements at night with a natural flicker. A civLevel 6 gate broadcasts CIVILIZATION_L6 and unlocks electronics, rocketry, aerospace, and nuclear physics tech branches. VelarSignalView.tsx presents a Morse-blink signal panel as a Level 7 teaser, triggering 30 seconds after the first rocket launch.

## 2. Application Overview

**What does this app do?**
It is a multiplayer 3D open-world survival game played entirely inside a web browser. Players explore a shared planet, manage survival stats (health, hunger, thirst, temperature, stamina), gather resources, craft items, smelt metals using real chemistry, build shelters, interact with NPC settlements, trade with a live supply-and-demand economy, commit crimes, hunt animals, sail waterways, fish, and advance through the Stone Age, Copper Age, and Iron Age. The world has four seasons with visual terrain effects and metabolic consequences. The world clock runs continuously even when no players are online.

**Who is it for?**
Players interested in open-world survival and crafting games, especially those who enjoy learning how the underlying systems actually work. The game's science grounding — real chemistry, real physics, real metabolic scaling — makes it appealing to players who want their actions to feel grounded rather than arbitrary.

**What problem does it solve?**
It provides a persistent shared survival world that is accessible without installing any software. All 3D graphics, physics simulation, and chemistry run inside the browser. The science grounding means players can learn real chemistry and metallurgy through play. The companion site at https://universe-companion.vercel.app extends this by explaining the science behind each mechanic in plain language.

**Technology stack (explained simply)**

| Technology | Plain-English Role |
|---|---|
| React | The framework that builds and updates the on-screen interface |
| Three.js / React Three Fiber | The 3D rendering engine that draws the world |
| Vite | Bundles the code so browsers can run it |
| TypeScript | Strict-typed JavaScript — catches mistakes before the game runs |
| Zustand | Stores and shares game state (vitals, position, inventory, etc.) |
| Clerk | Handles user login and session management |
| Neon Postgres | Cloud database for player saves, NPC memory, criminal records |
| Vercel | Hosts the frontend website, the HTTP API endpoints, and the companion site |
| Railway | Hosts the always-on WebSocket server that manages the live world |
| WebSocket | Persistent two-way connection for real-time multiplayer |
| Rapier3D | WebAssembly physics engine for character movement, collisions, and river current push |
| bitecs | Entity-component system managing player and creature data |
| Web Workers | Background threads running chemistry, thermal, fluid, and physics calculations |
| SharedArrayBuffer | Shared memory between workers and main thread for the simulation grid |

## 4. Features and Functionality

### Working Features — Complete and Verified

#### User Authentication
Players sign in with Clerk. The login screen shows the game title against a dark background. On load, the app checks authentication and either shows login or enters the game immediately. Player data loads from the Neon database after login and auto-saves every 60 seconds.

#### 3D World — Sphere Planet
The world is a procedurally generated sphere (cube-sphere geometry with fractional Brownian motion noise for elevation). It has:
- Real biome distribution based on height and latitude proxy
- Ocean (sea-blue vertices below sea level)
- Terrain with detail noise shader for surface micro-texture
- Atmospheric haze (fogExp2, density increases slightly at night)
- Stars rendered as a 5,000-point field, visible only when the sun is below the horizon
- Terrain shader pre-warmed via gl.compile() at startup to eliminate stutter on first render (M9)

#### Day/Night Cycle
A full 20-minute real-time day/night cycle is active. A THREE.Group containing the directional light and sun position rotates around the planet's Y-axis. The sky uses a Rayleigh scattering approximation — dawn and dusk produce warm orange tones, noon is cool blue, night is deep navy. Ambient light intensity follows a cosine curve (bright noon, dark midnight).

#### Fire System — Real Arrhenius Chemistry
Fire in this game is not a scripted visual. It is a genuine combustion reaction running inside a 3D grid of simulation cells:
- Each grid cell holds material type, temperature, density, and oxygen content
- The Arrhenius equation (k = A x exp(-Ea / RT)) governs how fast fuel burns at a given temperature
- Burning consumes oxygen and reduces cell density — fire can suffocate in a sealed space
- When cell density drops below 1 kg/m3, the cell converts to air (burnout)
- Six wood clusters are pre-ignited at world initialization so fire is visible immediately on spawn
- FireRenderer draws glowing sphere meshes and flickering point lights for all cells above 200 degrees C
- Fire photorealism: lights flicker via sin-wave plus noise, color gradients from deep red (300C) through orange (600C) to white-hot (1200C+), smoke plume particles above the flame
- The SimGrid Visualizer (press Tab) overlays a black-body color ramp on the terrain showing live temperature in every cell

#### River System (M9 Track 1)
Ten rivers flow across the planet, generated deterministically from the world seed:
- **Flow-field erosion**: RiverSystem.ts computes a vector flow field across the terrain, eroding channels 5 to 15 meters deep into the heightmap — these valleys are visible in the 3D terrain, not painted on
- **RiverRenderer.tsx**: Each river is a ribbon mesh (a flat strip of triangles following the channel) rendered with a custom Fresnel water shader — the water surface brightens at glancing angles and darkens when viewed straight-on, mimicking real water optics
- **KCC current push**: When a player wades into a river, the Rapier character controller receives a lateral impulse proportional to flow speed, pushing them downstream
- **Drinkable freshwater**: Pressing F near river water fills the hydration vital — replaces the need to carry water from puddles
- **Clay nodes**: Clay resource nodes spawn automatically on riverbanks, making clay a geographically meaningful resource (near water, not scattered randomly)
- **RiverHUD droplet indicator**: A small droplet icon appears in the HUD when the player is standing close enough to a river to drink from it

#### Animal AI System (M9 Track 2)
Three species of animals roam the world, each with distinct behavior:

**Deer**
- Flock together in groups using a separation/cohesion/alignment rule set
- Flee from players and wolves when within detection range
- Passive until provoked — will not attack

**Wolves**
- Travel in packs and coordinate hunts
- Hunt deer: one wolf drives prey toward the pack, others flank
- Attack players who come too close or who enter pack territory
- Drop wolf_pelt on death

**Boars**
- Solitary or in small groups
- Charge the player if threatened — a straight-line burst of speed toward the target
- More aggressive than deer, less coordinated than wolves
- Drop boar_tusk on death

**AnimalRenderer.tsx**: All animals use Three.js InstancedMesh — every animal of the same species is drawn in a single GPU draw call. Geometry is procedural (no external model files required). A three-tier LOD system activates automatically: full-detail up close, reduced polygon count at medium range, billboard at far distance.

**Ecosystem population caps**: Each species has a maximum population enforced globally. New animals do not spawn if the cap is reached, preventing runaway populations.

**Hunting loot table**:

| Animal | Loot Drops |
|---|---|
| Deer | raw_meat, hide (used for leather) |
| Wolf | raw_meat, wolf_pelt |
| Boar | raw_meat, bone, boar_tusk |

**New crafting recipes unlocked by M9 animal loot**:

| Item | Materials | Unlocks |
|---|---|---|
| Leather | 2x hide (process at fire) | Armor tier |
| Leather Armor | 5x leather + 2x bone needle | Reduces incoming damage |
| Bone Needle | 1x bone | Required for leather crafting |
| Tusk Knife | 1x boar_tusk + 1x bone | A fast melee weapon with bleed effect |

#### Survival Loop — All 7 Slices Verified

**Slice 1: Gather**
Press F near a resource node to collect it to inventory. A live hotbar displays the first 5 inventory slots with item names, updated at 200 ms polling. Resource nodes include: stone, flint, wood, clay (now found on riverbanks), fiber, copper ore, iron ore, coal, tin ore, sand, sulfur, bark, herbs, and more. Nodes respawn after 60 seconds. Visual feedback: node shrinks and disappears with a particle burst.

**Slice 2: Craft and Chop**
Hold rock and flint, press C to open crafting. Craft a stone tool, equip it from inventory. Approach a tree and attack — a 3-hit system with health bars visible above resource nodes. On the third hit, the tree yields wood and disappears.

**Slice 3: Light Fire**
Gather tinder and wood. Equip flint. Press attack near a wood node. The game calls localSim.placeWood() then localSim.ignite() with 50,000 joules — enough to exceed wood's ignition temperature and start real Arrhenius combustion.

**Slice 4: Cook and Eat**
Place raw food near an active fire cell (above 200 degrees C). The sim thermodynamics system checks proximity to hot cells over time and converts raw food to cooked. Eating cooked food restores the hunger (satiety) vital bar. Cooked food restores significantly more than raw food. Raw_meat from animals can also be cooked here.

**Slice 5: Treat Wounds**
Taking combat or environmental damage creates a wound with a bacterial load. The logistic growth equation governs how that load grows over time. Gathering herbs and applying them to the wound reduces bacterial count. Once bacterial load drops to zero, the wound heals.

**Slice 6: Build Shelter and Sleep**
Craft a bedroll (3 Fiber + 2 Wood). Place it in the world. Approach and press E to sleep. During sleep, stamina restores. The bedroll also sets the player's respawn anchor (see Death system below).

**Slice 7: Smelt Copper and Craft Copper Tools**
The full copper smelting chain:
1. Gather copper ore (Cu2S) and coal (carbon source)
2. Build a basic furnace from stone and clay
3. Light a fire beneath the furnace
4. The chemistry engine runs the real reduction: Cu2S + C + heat -> Cu + SO2
5. Copper metal appears in the furnace output
6. Craft copper knife, axe, or pickaxe with the smithingXp quality system

#### Iron Age (M7)
Iron ore appears on mid-elevation terrain following sedimentary placement rules (not random scattering). The full iron chain:
1. Mine iron ore — requires an iron pickaxe, so copper tools must come first
2. Build a blast furnace (8 stone + 4 clay) — requires temperatures above 1000 degrees C to operate
3. Run the real iron reduction: Fe2O3 + 3C -> 2Fe + 3CO2
4. Craft iron knife, axe, and pickaxe with the smithing experience quality system
5. Settlement civLevel 2 triggers a SETTLEMENT_UNLOCKED_IRON broadcast to all players

#### Shared World (M5)
- Server-authoritative resource depletion: NODE_DESTROYED broadcasts to all players so no two players can gather the same node
- Fire ignition syncs via WebSocket: FIRE_STARTED is relayed to all clients
- New players joining receive a WORLD_SNAPSHOT with the depleted node list so the world is consistent immediately
- Player nameplates show Clerk usernames above each player's head
- All resource node health and fire states are authoritative on the server
- WebSocket batch updates via BATCH_UPDATE message type reduce per-frame network overhead (M9)

#### Death System
- YOU DIED overlay appears on death
- Inventory drops to the ground as world-pickable loot
- Player respawns at their placed bedroll, or at the world spawn point if no bedroll exists
- Respawn vitals are groggy (reduced but not empty) to create a recovery window
- Criminal record: murder_count is stored in Neon DB and persists through death and reconnection

#### NPC Settlements (M6)
Five named settlements exist in the world:

| Settlement | Approximate Location |
|---|---|
| Ashford | Plains near spawn |
| Ironhaven | Mid-elevation, near iron deposits |
| Saltmere | Coastal lowlands |
| Thornwall | Forested highlands |
| Ridgepost | Rocky ridge terrain |

Each settlement:
- Advances civLevel by crafting and researching (same chemistry rules as players)
- Has NPC memory in Neon DB tracking trust and threat score per player
- Controls a 150m territory radius that NPCs patrol and defend
- Has a trade system: TRADE_OFFER -> TRADE_ACCEPT -> inventory transfer
- Closes its gate when a player's threat score exceeds 3
- Shows a SettlementHUD with reaction banners (Friendly / Wary / Hostile)

#### PvP and Outlaw System (M7)
- Kill detection fires PLAYER_KILLED WebSocket message
- murder_count increments in Neon DB on each kill
- Tiered NPC reactions:
  - 1-2 murders: NPCs are wary, reduced trade prices
  - 3-4 murders: settlement gates close, NPCs will not trade
  - 5+ murders: active bounty placed, WANTED label with bounty amount appears above the player's head
- Bounty collection: killing a wanted player transfers the copper reward to the killer's inventory
- Redemption quests: escort, delivery, and defense missions reduce murder_count
- Criminal record survives death: "Your crimes are remembered" shows on the death screen

#### Photorealism (M5 pass)
Visual systems added in the M5 photorealism pass:
- Wet terrain edges: albedo darkens and roughness increases within 20m of sea level
- Rock specular: highlight variation by face angle
- Creature subsurface scattering: a cheap backlit translucency term in the fragment shader
- Tree wind sway: vertex shader wind animation at 0.5 Hz, 2 cm amplitude
- Post-processing: Bloom (glow on fires and emissive materials) + Vignette (edge darkening)

#### Organisms (M0)
Ten organisms spawn from the genome encoder at world initialization. They wander with a simple AI and are rendered with subsurface scattering. The genome encoder produces varied phenotypes; organisms are not identical.

#### Weather System (M8)
WeatherRenderer is built and active in the scene:
- A Markov-chain system transitions between CLEAR, CLOUDY, RAIN, STORM, and SNOW weather states
- Rain: 2,000 instanced line segments falling with wind-direction velocity
- Snow: 800 instanced billboard quads with slow spiral descent (activates when temperature < 0 degrees C)
- Storm: random lightning flashes on a 15-45 second interval
- Wind: 300 dust/leaf sprites in CLEAR and CLOUDY states
- Clouds: billboard quad mesh at horizon level with opacity driven by weather state
- Fire extinguishing during RAIN/STORM is in progress

#### Seasons System (M10 Track A)

The world now has a four-season year driven by SeasonSystem.js:

- A full year lasts 40 real-time minutes, divided evenly: 10 minutes per season (spring, summer, autumn, winter).
- SeasonalTerrainPass.tsx applies a visual overlay on top of the terrain shader each season:
  - Autumn: amber tint on foliage areas
  - Winter: white snow layer blended over ground surfaces
  - Spring: pastel blossom particles near tree vertices
  - Summer: no overlay (baseline appearance)
- Winter metabolic penalty: all hunger drain rates increase by +20% during winter, reflecting the real caloric cost of thermoregulation in cold conditions. Players must keep food supplies higher in winter to avoid starvation.
- SeasonWidget appears in the HUD showing the current season name and the current day of the year (e.g., "Autumn — Day 23 of 40").

#### Sailing and Fishing System (M10 Track B)

Two craftable watercraft are now available, plus a complete fishing system:

**Watercraft**

| Item | ID | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Raft | ITEM 59 | A simple wind-driven flat craft. Moves in the wind direction only. Easy to build, limited maneuverability. |
| Sailing Boat | ITEM 60 | A full sailing vessel with tacking mechanics. Can sail at angles to the wind using SailingSystem.ts. |

SailingSystem.ts implements real sailing physics:
- Wind direction is read from the WeatherRenderer wind state.
- The sailing boat can tack (zigzag upwind) but has a no-go zone — a 45-degree arc directly into the wind where the sail cannot fill and the boat loses drive.
- The raft is simpler: it moves with the wind and cannot sail upwind at all.
- SailingRenderer.tsx draws both vessels as procedural meshes — no external model files required.

**Navigation**

| Item | ID | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Compass | ITEM 61 | Displays magnetic bearing relative to the planet's north pole. Shown as a needle overlay in the HUD while held. |

**Fishing**

| Item | ID | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Fishing Rod | ITEM 62 | Cast from the shore or from a watercraft while near a river or coast. |

Fishing mechanic: cast the rod with the use key, then wait. A bite timer ticks randomly between 5 and 15 seconds. When a bite registers, a prompt appears and the player must press the use key again within the reaction window to land the catch. Fish is a new food material (MAT 60) that can be eaten raw (low nutrition) or cooked over fire (full nutrition).

**New crafting recipes (IDs 81-87)**

| Recipe ID | Item | Materials |
|---|---|---|
| 81 | Raft | 8 Wood + 4 Rope + 2 Fiber |
| 82 | Sailing Boat | 20 Wood + 8 Rope + 4 Cloth + 2 Iron |
| 83 | Compass | 1 Iron + 1 Copper + 1 Glass |
| 84 | Fishing Rod | 2 Wood + 3 Fiber + 1 Rope |
| 85 | Cooked Fish | 1 Fish (cook over fire) |
| 86 | Salt | Evaporate seawater (Saltmere specialization) |
| 87 | Smoked Fish | 1 Fish + 1 Salt (preserve at fire) |

#### Trade Economy (M10 Track C)

The five NPC settlements now operate a live supply-and-demand economy persisted to Neon DB:

**How pricing works**

TradeEconomy.js manages a settlement_stockpiles table in Neon DB. Each settlement holds a quantity of each good it produces and buys. When supply of a good is high, the price the settlement pays falls (minimum 0.6x base price). When supply is low, price rises (maximum 2.5x base price). This means player trading behavior actually changes the market — selling a large volume of fish to Saltmere will depress the fish price until the stockpile is drawn down.

**Settlement specializations**

| Settlement | Produces / Sells at Discount | Buys at Premium |
|---|---|---|
| Ashford | Wood, Leather | Stone, Grain |
| Ironhaven | Metals (copper, iron) | Coal, Stone |
| Saltmere | Fish, Salt | Wood, Grain |
| Thornwall | Stone, Ore | Leather, Food |
| Ridgepost | Food, Grain | Salt, Metals |

Specialization means each settlement's home goods are always well-stocked and therefore cheaper to buy, while imported goods are scarce and priced at a premium.

**NPC caravans**

Every 5 real-time minutes a caravan spawns at a settlement and walks to another, transferring a payload of trade goods. This gradually rebalances stockpiles across settlements and means prices are dynamic even when no player is trading. Players who intercept a caravan route and trade at the right moment can profit from price differentials.

**ShopHUD**

ShopHUD.tsx provides the trade interface when a player enters a settlement and opens the shop:
- A Buy tab showing all goods the settlement currently sells, with live prices that update from the stockpile database.
- A Sell tab showing all goods the settlement is currently buying, with live prices.
- Transactions use copper coins (MAT 59) as currency.

**New materials**

| Material | ID | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Copper Coin | MAT 59 | Minted at settlements; currency for all trade transactions |
| Fish | MAT 60 | Caught with a Fishing Rod at rivers or coast |
| Salt | MAT 61 | Produced at Saltmere; used in Smoked Fish recipe |
| Grain | MAT 62 | Produced at Ridgepost; sold as food |

**Next available IDs at M10 completion**: MAT 63, ITEM 63, Recipe 88

#### Gunpowder Chemistry (M11)

KNO3 + C + S now runs as a real Arrhenius combustion reaction in the chemistry grid. The three reagents must reach threshold temperature together before the reaction ignites — the same physical logic that governs wood fire.

**New materials and sourcing**

| Material | ID | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Saltpeter (KNO3) | MAT 63 | Forms in nitrogen-rich soil near animal waste deposits and cave floors |
| Sulfur | MAT 64 | Found in volcanic terrain zones |
| Gunpowder | MAT 65 (ROCKET_FUEL) | Combine KNO3 + Carbon + Sulfur at the correct Arrhenius temperature |

**New item**

| Item | ID | Recipe | Unlock |
|---|---|---|---|
| Musket | ITEM 63 | 1 Iron Barrel + 1 Powder Charge | civLevel 3+ |

The iron barrel is a sub-component crafted from iron ingots. The powder charge is loaded with gunpowder. The musket is the first ranged projectile weapon in the game that uses chemical propulsion rather than spring or bow mechanics.

#### Castle Structures (M11)

A multi-block construction system for fortifications:

- **Wall segment**: stacks horizontally and vertically to build curtain walls
- **Tower**: corner and inline tower blocks with elevated firing positions
- **Gate**: the gatehouse block that opens and closes as a player-operated door

CastleRenderer.tsx handles all three block types as a unified renderer. Siege mechanics allow attackers to batter gates and walls with structural damage. City walls become available to NPC settlements at civLevel 4 and player-constructed castles are unlocked at civLevel 5.

#### Astronomy (M11)

A real star particle system using actual IAU constellation position data. Visible only when the sun is below the horizon (same condition as the existing 5,000-point star field, which this system augments or replaces).

| Item | ID | Recipe | Requirement |
|---|---|---|---|
| Glass Ingot | MAT 66 | Sand + heat (kiln) | civLevel 2 |
| Telescope | ITEM 64 | 4 Iron Ingot + 2 Glass Ingot | civLevel 3 |

While the telescope is equipped and the player looks at the night sky, a HUD overlay labels visible constellations. Moon phase is tracked against the world clock. A next-eclipse time is computed from orbital mechanics and displayed in the astronomy HUD.

#### Diplomacy System (M11)

- **Mayor NPCs**: each settlement appoints the highest-trust NPC as mayor. The mayor NPC has special dialogue options and speaks authoritatively for the settlement.
- **Diplomatic envoys**: when two settlements decide to open relations, a named envoy NPC walks from one settlement to the other. The journey is visible in the world.
- **War and peace declarations**: settlements can formally declare war or sign peace. A player's criminal alignment shifts based on which side they support — attacking an enemy of an allied settlement is treated differently from attacking an ally.

#### Space Age — Rocket System (M12)

RocketSystem.ts implements a three-phase launch sequence:

1. **Countdown** — a launch sequence timer counts down from T-10 seconds.
2. **Ignition** — rocket fuel ignites in the chemistry grid; exhaust begins.
3. **Ascent** — the rocket accelerates upward; ROCKET_LAUNCHED WebSocket message is broadcast to all clients with a screen shake effect.

RocketVFXRenderer.tsx renders the visual effect as three concentric exhaust layers:
- Inner cone: white-hot core (high-temperature combustion products)
- Middle cone: orange-amber glow (partially burned gases)
- Outer cone: dark soot dispersal (unburned particulate)

Total particle budget: 180 particles per active rocket. A ShaderMaterial heat shimmer distorts geometry above the exhaust cone. A circular scorch disc appears on the terrain at the launch pad after liftoff.

#### Space Age — Radio System (M12)

RadioSystem.ts manages a persistent broadcast queue and a registry of all constructed radio towers. When a player activates a tower, the PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST WebSocket message is sent to all connected clients.

RadioTowerVFXRenderer.tsx draws teal electromagnetic pulse rings using additive blending. One ring emits every two seconds, expanding outward and fading before the next appears. This makes active towers visually distinct in the world at a distance.

#### Space Age — Electric Lighting (M12)

ElectricLightPass.tsx activates when a settlement reaches civLevel 6. It places 40 Three.js PointLights above settlement buildings:
- Color temperature: 2700K warm white (#FFBA74)
- Visible only at night (linked to the DayNightCycle sun position)
- A natural flicker is applied to each light to simulate filament behavior

This makes civLevel 6 settlements visible from across the planet at night and provides a clear visual signal of technological advancement.

#### Space Age — civLevel 6 Gate and L7 Teaser (M12)

When a settlement crosses the civLevel 6 threshold, a CIVILIZATION_L6 WebSocket broadcast fires. This unlocks four new technology branches in the tech tree: electronics, rocketry, aerospace, and nuclear physics.

VelarSignalView.tsx renders a signal panel with Morse-blink animation. It activates 30 seconds after the first ROCKET_LAUNCHED event in a session. The panel displays a cryptic repeating signal pattern and teaser text pointing toward Level 7 content. This is a narrative hook for the next milestone.

**New material and item IDs (M11-M12)**

| ID | Type | Name |
|---|---|---|
| MAT 63 | Material | Saltpeter (KNO3) |
| MAT 64 | Material | Sulfur (volcanic) |
| MAT 65 | Material | Rocket Fuel (gunpowder blend) |
| MAT 66 | Material | Circuit Board |
| MAT 67 | Material | Nuclear Fuel |
| ITEM 63 | Item | Musket |
| ITEM 64 | Item | Telescope |
| ITEM 65 | Item | Artillery Shell |
| Recipe 88-99 | Recipes | M11-M12 crafting (gunpowder, musket, telescope, castle blocks, circuits, rocket fuel) |

**Next available IDs**: MAT 68, ITEM 66, Recipe 100

#### Science Companion Site (M9)
The companion site is live at https://universe-companion.vercel.app. The Science panel button (S key) in the sidebar now links to this URL. The site explains the real science behind the game's mechanics in plain language.

#### Save and Load

- Auto-save every 60 seconds

#### Admin Controls
For the designated administrator only:

- Time controls: pause/resume, time scale from 0.1x to 1,000,000x

## 5. User Interface and Design

**Visual Theme**
The UI uses a consistent visual language: dark near-black backgrounds, semi-transparent frosted panels with blur effect, white monospace typography, rust-orange and blue accent colors. The HUD has a Rust-style design with icon + horizontal fill bars for each vital. Warning states glow red.

**Layout**
- Top-left: Vitals panel (always visible during play) — five vital bars with icons, hotbar below, ambient temperature display, RiverHUD droplet indicator when near freshwater, SeasonWidget showing current season and day of year, astronomy overlay (constellation labels and moon phase) when telescope is equipped at night
- Top-right: Clock and connection status, offset to avoid icon strip
- Right edge: 48px icon strip with 8 panel buttons
- Full viewport: 3D canvas behind the UI
- Center: Crosshair when pointer is locked; click-to-play overlay when not
- Bottom-right: Admin button (admin accounts only)
- On-screen HUD: SettlementHUD reaction banners, WANTED labels above outlaw players, player nameplates

**HUD Vitals**
Five bars in the Rust icon-plus-fill style:
- Health (red heart icon) — drops on damage, starvation, dehydration
- Satiety (food icon) — hunger building, empties in ~20 minutes
- Hydration (water drop icon) — thirst, empties in ~12 minutes; rivers now replenish this
- Energy (lightning icon) — drains with exertion
- Stamina (sleep icon) — depletes with prolonged activity, restored by sleep

**RiverHUD Droplet Indicator**
A small water droplet icon appears in the HUD when the player is within drinking distance of a river. This makes freshwater access clearly readable without breaking immersion.

**Hotbar**
Five inventory slots displayed permanently below the vitals. Updated at 200 ms polling. Shows item name (abbreviated to fit the 52px slot width). Active slot highlighted.

**Ambient Temperature Display**
Below the vitals: "AMBIENT: XX.X C" color-coded:
- Blue: below 0 degrees C
- Green: 0-30 degrees C (normal)
- Orange: 30-50 degrees C (hot)
- Red: 50+ degrees C (dangerous)

**Responsiveness**
The game is designed for desktop browsers. It requires pointer lock (mouse look), WASD keyboard input, and a wide panel layout (480px). Not usable on touch devices.

**Panel Animation**
Panels slide in from the right over 250 ms. When any panel opens, the mouse cursor is released and game input is blocked.

**Notifications**
Toast notifications fire for: crafting success/failure, harvest events, fire started, death, respawn, bounty collected, redemption quest complete.

---

## 6. Performance Analysis

**Rendering**
- Three.js canvas fills the entire viewport with antialiasing and high-performance GPU preference
- Shadow maps at 2048x2048 — high quality, GPU-intensive
- Sphere planet uses FBM noise terrain, detail noise shader, ocean + atmosphere
- Post-processing: Bloom + Vignette passes active
- 5,000 stars via Stars component
- Remote players and NPCs are simple geometric shapes (efficient)
- Instanced geometry for weather particles: no per-frame allocations
- Fire cells: one draw call + one point light per burning cell. A maximum point light budget may be needed for large fires
- AnimalRenderer: InstancedMesh for all three animal species — one draw call per species regardless of population count (M9)
- Creature LOD: three tiers (full detail / reduced polygon / billboard) selected automatically by distance from camera (M9)
- Terrain shader warmed up via gl.compile() at startup — eliminates visible stutter on the first frame the terrain shader is used (M9)

**Simulation Grid**
- Chemistry worker at 10 Hz in a separate thread — does not block rendering
- Thermal worker at 10 Hz: per-material heat diffusion (M9 — was unspecified before)
- Fluid/flow worker at 20 Hz: river flow-field simulation (M9 new)
- Physics worker: 60 Hz Rapier3D physics
- Fire renderer polls at 10 Hz via delta accumulator in useFrame

**Network**
- Player position: 10 updates/second per client
- Server to client: 10 Hz WORLD_SNAPSHOT
- Non-critical world state updates batched into BATCH_UPDATE messages to reduce per-frame WebSocket traffic (M9)
- Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff

**UI Pooling**
- NodeHealthBars use a pre-allocated 32-slot pool (M9). Health bar objects are reused rather than created and destroyed per node interaction, reducing garbage collection pressure.

**Potential Performance Concerns**
- Shadow camera frustum covers the full planet — may stall on integrated graphics or older hardware
- Large simultaneous fires: proportional increase in point lights
- Weather particles add ~3,100 instances when active — budget is reasonable but not yet benchmarked at scale
- Rivers add 10 ribbon meshes and a flow-field worker at 20 Hz — memory impact is small but the fluid worker adds continuous CPU load on a dedicated thread
- SeasonalTerrainPass introduces a full-terrain shader blend on season change — one-time GPU cost per transition, not per frame
- SailingRenderer adds two procedural mesh objects when watercraft are spawned — low impact; meshes are disposed on craft destruction
- TradeEconomy NPC caravan updates and stockpile reads hit Neon DB every 5 minutes per caravan — minor but should be monitored at scale with many simultaneous caravans
- CastleRenderer (M11): castle blocks are static meshes instanced by block type — cost scales with player-constructed fortification size
- RocketVFXRenderer (M12): 180 particles active during ascent phase plus a ShaderMaterial heat shimmer — GPU cost is one-time per launch event, not per frame at rest
- RadioTowerVFXRenderer (M12): additive-blend pulse rings at 1 ring per 2 seconds per tower — low cost; scales with number of constructed towers
- ElectricLightPass (M12): 40 PointLights per civLevel 6 settlement, night-only — Three.js PointLights are among the most GPU-expensive light types; performance should be monitored when multiple settlements reach civLevel 6 simultaneously

---

## 7. Accessibility Review

The application has limited accessibility support appropriate for a mouse-and-keyboard 3D survival game.

**What works**
- Sidebar icon buttons include aria-label attributes
- Icon buttons use aria-pressed to indicate active state
- Close buttons on panels have aria-label="Close panel"
- Time control buttons include aria-label and aria-pressed
- Vital bars use percentage text labels alongside color, not color alone
- Temperature display uses both color and a numeric value

**What is missing or problematic**
- The game requires pointer lock — no keyboard-only play mode
- No focus management when panels open
- No focus trap inside panels — Tab cycles the whole page while a panel is open
- Secondary text color contrast may fail WCAG AA requirements
- No reduced-motion support — animations play regardless of system settings

**WCAG Compliance**: Approximately Level A for labeled UI elements. The 3D gameplay is inaccessible by design given the medium.

---

## 8. Security Assessment

**Authentication**
Clerk handles all authentication. Vercel API routes verify the Clerk JWT on every request using CLERK_SECRET_KEY. This is a correct and secure pattern.

**Authorization**
- /api/save and /api/load verify that the requesting user matches the data being accessed
- /api/world-settings POST and /api/admin verify the admin user ID server-side
- The admin button is hidden client-side using VITE_ADMIN_USER_ID — this is UI convenience only; real security is on the server

**Identified Risks**

| Risk | Severity | Explanation |
|---|---|---|
| ADMIN_SET_TIME WebSocket command has no authentication | Medium | Any client connected to the Railway WebSocket can send ADMIN_SET_TIME and change the world clock for all players. The server does not verify admin identity. |
| admin.ts still uses Web API style (Request/Response) | Medium | This endpoint was not converted during the API fix session. It returns 500 errors in Vercel's Node.js runtime. The admin player list will fail to load. |
| Status site connects to WebSocket with no authentication | Low | The status page joins the server as "__status_viewer__". It receives the same WORLD_SNAPSHOT as any player. No sensitive data beyond position and health is exposed, but the feed is unauthenticated. |
| No rate limiting on save/load endpoints | Low | A malicious user could spam /api/save. Vercel's default limits provide some protection but no explicit rate limit is configured. |
| discoveries stored as raw JSON string | Low | Stored with JSON.stringify in a TEXT column rather than a native JSONB column. Works but fragile if queried directly. |
| /api/init-db has no authentication | Low | Anyone who knows the URL can call it. Safe to call repeatedly (uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) but should be removed or protected after setup. |

---

## 9. Code Quality

**Overall Assessment**: The code is well-organized and readable. The separation between frontend, API layer, and game server is clean. The simulation modules (src/sim/) are pure functions with no side effects, making them easy to test and reason about. The new systems added in M9 (RiverSystem.ts, AnimalAISystem.ts) follow the same pattern as earlier systems — self-contained, domain-scoped, with no cross-contamination between concerns. The game loop in SceneRoot.tsx is complex but well-commented. Zustand stores are small and focused.

**Strengths**
- TypeScript throughout, consistent strict mode
- Zustand stores are small and domain-scoped: player, game, multiplayer, UI, save, settlement, outlaw, weather
- WorldSocket class handles reconnection logic correctly with no memory leaks
- WorldClock uses wall-clock delta time, preventing time drift on process delay
- MaterialRegistry, GridCoords, Arrhenius are pure functions with zero side effects
- All simulation modules use real physical constants from constants.ts
- Chemistry worker uses a clean message-passing protocol (IGNITE, PLACE_MATERIAL, TICK)
- SurvivalSystems.ts covers all 7 slices cleanly without dead-end UI stubs
- DeathSystem correctly drops inventory as world pickups and respawns at bedroll anchor
- SettlementStore, OutlawStore, WeatherStore are each self-contained with no cross-contamination
- WeatherRenderer: all particle geometry is instanced, no per-frame allocations
- Status site is fully isolated in src/status/ — no dependencies on game stores, auth, or 3D libraries
- Save operation uses SQL upsert (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) — correct pattern
- AnimalRenderer uses InstancedMesh — zero per-animal draw calls (M9)
- NodeHealthBars pool is pre-allocated (32 slots) — zero GC pressure during node interactions (M9)
- fluidWorker at 20 Hz runs river flow-field on its own thread — does not block rendering (M9)

**Weaknesses**
- admin.ts uses the old Web API Request/Response style while other routes use Node.js req/res — a real production failure
- Four of eight panels remain empty placeholders (Tech Tree, Evolution, Journal, Settings)
- InventoryPanel uses a useState counter (forceRefresh) as a manual refresh trigger — the inventory singleton is not reactive state
- saveGame() does not notify the user on failure
- Test coverage exists only for the three sim modules — the rest of the codebase is untested

**Testing**
- Vitest is configured
- 18 tests covering MaterialRegistry (4), GridCoords (8), and Arrhenius (6) — all passing
- No test coverage for survival systems, API routes, WebSocket behavior, NPC systems, settlement logic, RiverSystem, or AnimalAISystem

## 11. APIs and Integrations

### Vercel API Routes (HTTP)

| Endpoint | Method | Auth Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| /api/save | POST | Yes | Upserts player state to player_saves |
| /api/load | GET | Yes | Retrieves player state from player_saves |
| /api/world-settings | GET | No | Returns time_scale from world_settings |
| /api/world-settings | POST | Yes (admin) | Updates time_scale in world_settings |
| /api/admin | GET | Yes (admin) | Returns all player records |
| /api/init-db | GET | No | Creates database tables — one-time setup |

### Railway WebSocket Server (Real-time)

**Client to Server messages**:

| Message Type | When Sent | Payload |
|---|---|---|
| JOIN | On connection | userId, username |
| PLAYER_UPDATE | 10 Hz (game clients) | userId, x, y, z, health |
| NODE_DESTROYED | When player gathers a node | nodeId |
| FIRE_STARTED | When player lights fire | position x/y/z |
| PLAYER_KILLED | When player kills another | killerId, victimId |
| TRADE_OFFER | Player initiates trade with NPC | settlementId, offeredItems |
| TRADE_ACCEPT | NPC accepts trade | tradeDelta |
| SHOP_BUY | Player buys from settlement shop | settlementId, materialId, quantity |
| SHOP_SELL | Player sells to settlement shop | settlementId, materialId, quantity |
| ADMIN_SET_TIME | Admin changes time scale | timeScale, paused |

**Server to Client messages**:

| Message Type | When Sent | Payload |
|---|---|---|
| WORLD_SNAPSHOT | 10 Hz to all clients | simTime, timeScale, paused, epoch, all players, all NPCs |
| BATCH_UPDATE | As needed | Batched non-critical world state deltas (M9) |
| PLAYER_JOINED | New player connects | player data |
| PLAYER_LEFT | Player disconnects | userId |
| NODE_DESTROYED | Resource node depleted | nodeId, destroyerId |
| FIRE_STARTED | Fire ignited | x, y, z |
| PLAYER_KILLED | Player death event | killerId, victimId, bountyAwarded |
| SETTLEMENT_UNLOCKED_IRON | Settlement reaches civLevel 2 | settlementId |
| BOUNTY_COLLECTED | Bounty claimed | collectorId, amount |
| SEASON_CHANGED | Season transitions | season, dayOfYear |
| STOCKPILE_UPDATE | Settlement stockpile changed after a shop transaction or caravan delivery | settlementId, materialId, newQuantity |
| CARAVAN_SPAWNED | NPC caravan begins journey | fromSettlement, toSettlement, goods |
| ROCKET_LAUNCHED | First rocket launch event (M12) | launchSitePosition, timestamp |
| PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST | Player activates a radio tower (M12) | towerId, playerId, message |
| CIVILIZATION_L6 | Settlement reaches civLevel 6 (M12) | settlementId |

### Clerk (Authentication)
Provides sign-in UI, manages sessions, issues JWTs. Frontend uses useAuth() and useUser(). Backend uses verifyToken(). Status site does not use Clerk.

### Neon Postgres (Database)
Serverless Postgres via @neondatabase/serverless. SQL queries use tagged template literals for automatic input sanitization.

### Universe Companion Site (M9)
A separate Vercel deployment at https://universe-companion.vercel.app. The Science panel (S key) links directly to this URL. The site explains the real science behind each game mechanic in plain language.

---

## 12. Data and Database

### Tables

**player_saves** — one row per player

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| user_id | TEXT (primary key) | Clerk user ID |
| username | TEXT | Display name |
| pos_x, pos_y, pos_z | REAL | Last known world position |
| health, hunger, thirst, energy, fatigue | REAL | Vitals (0 to 1) |
| ev_points | INTEGER | Accumulated evolution points |
| civ_tier | INTEGER | Current civilization tier |
| discoveries | TEXT | JSON array of discovery IDs |
| current_goal | TEXT | Active high-level goal |
| sim_seconds | REAL | Cumulative simulation time |
| murder_count | INTEGER | Criminal record — persists through death |
| updated_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | Last save timestamp |

**world_settings** — a single row

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| id | Integer | Always 1 |
| time_scale | REAL | Current simulation speed multiplier |
| sim_time | REAL | Authoritative simulation clock in seconds |
| updated_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | Last write timestamp |

**npc_memory** — one row per (NPC / player) pair

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| settlement_id | TEXT | Settlement identifier |
| player_id | TEXT | Clerk user ID |
| trust | REAL | Trust score (-1 to 1) |
| threat | INTEGER | Threat level (0-5+) |
| last_interaction | TIMESTAMPTZ | When the NPC last encountered this player |

**settlement_stockpiles** — one row per (settlement / material) pair (M10)

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| settlement_id | TEXT | Settlement identifier |
| material_id | INTEGER | Material ID from the material registry |
| quantity | REAL | Current stockpile quantity |
| updated_at | TIMESTAMPTZ | Last write timestamp |

Price is computed at query time from quantity relative to a per-settlement baseline — no separate price column is stored.

### Data Concerns
- discoveries stored as a serialized JSON string in TEXT rather than native JSONB — cannot be indexed or queried directly
- No migration system — schema changes require manual SQL
- Simulation grid (fire, heat, river flow) is client-side only — not persisted. Active fires are lost on disconnect
- The npc_memory table enables genuine NPC memory across sessions and servers — this is a significant feature
- settlement_stockpiles requires the init-db endpoint to be called (or the table created manually) for M10 trade economy to function — no automated migration exists

---

## 13. Deployment and Infrastructure

### Frontend (Vercel)

React application built with Vite, deployed to Vercel. Two named entry points in vite.config.ts:
- `main` — the game, at index.html
- `status` — the dashboard, at status/index.html

Both compile in a single build pass and deploy together.

**Required browser headers** (vercel.json):
- Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy: same-origin — required for SharedArrayBuffer
- Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy: require-corp — required alongside the above

These headers are essential. Without them, Rapier3D physics fails silently and the chemistry grid cannot use shared memory.

### API Layer (Vercel Serverless Functions)

The api/ directory deploys automatically as serverless functions. Each TypeScript file becomes an HTTP endpoint on Node.js with auto-parsed JSON bodies.

### Game Server (Railway)

Always-on Node.js process:
- Starts at boot, loads settings from database, begins ticking the world clock
- Accepts WebSocket connections on configurable port (default 8080)
- Broadcasts WORLD_SNAPSHOT at 10 Hz and BATCH_UPDATE messages as needed (M9)
- Persists sim time and time scale to database every 30 seconds
- Restarts automatically via Railway process supervision

### Science Companion Site (Vercel)
A separate Vercel project deployed at https://universe-companion.vercel.app. Live as of M9.

### Environment Variables Required

| Variable | Where Used | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| DATABASE_URL | API routes + Railway server | Neon Postgres connection string |
| CLERK_SECRET_KEY | API routes | Verifies Clerk JWTs server-side |
| ADMIN_USER_ID | API routes | Admin account Clerk user ID |
| VITE_ADMIN_USER_ID | Frontend build | Shows/hides admin button client-side |
| VITE_WS_URL | Frontend build | WebSocket URL for Railway server |

---

## 14. Status Website — Mission Control Dashboard

### What It Is

A live, public-facing dashboard accessible at /status/ — no login, no account required. It shows where players are, what NPCs are doing, what era the simulation is in, and whether the server is running. Updates live at 10 Hz from the same Railway WebSocket server that powers the game.

### How to Access It

Navigate to https://universe-sim-beryl.vercel.app/status/ in any browser.

### Layout

```
+----------------------------------------------------------+
|  UNIVERSE SIM  MISSION CONTROL  |  STELLAR ERA  |  SYNC  |  <- EpochBar
+------------------------------------------+---------------+
|                                          |               |
|                                          |  SERVER       |
|           SATELLITE MAP                  |  STATUS       |
|         (canvas, live positions)         |               |
|                                          |  SIDEBAR      |
|                                          |               |
+------------------------------------------+---------------+
|  ACTIVE AGENTS  [count]                                  |  <- PlayerRoster
|  [card] [card] [card] ...                                |
+----------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Components

**EpochBar** — Top strip showing app identity, current cosmological epoch (flashes gold on era transition), world time, time scale, PAUSED/LIVE badge, and SYNC/OFFLINE indicator.

**SatelliteMap** — Main panel. An HTML canvas (800x600 internal resolution) rendering:
- Procedural terrain generated with fractional Brownian motion once at load
- NPC dots color-coded by behavior state (green=wander, amber=gather, bright green=eat, blue=rest, purple=socialize)
- Player dots with pulsing rings and a color-coded health arc (green above 50%, orange 25-50%, red below 25%)
- Username labels above each player
- Coordinate grid, N/S/E/W compass, central crosshair, blinking LIVE indicator
- Click on a player dot to open their detail card

**ServerStats** — Right sidebar: connection status, players online count, NPCs active, NPC behavior breakdown (mini bar chart), world-forming progress bar (during bootstrap), plain-English epoch description.

**PlayerRoster** — Bottom strip: scrollable player cards showing initials avatar, username, health bar, and coordinates. Clickable to open detail.

**PlayerDetail** — Slide-up modal on player click: initials avatar, username, Agent ID, health bar (glows red below 25%), X/Y/Z coordinates.

### Design Aesthetic
Near-black background (#060810), animated scrolling starfield, cyan accents (#00d4ff), IBM Plex Mono for data readouts, Orbitron for headings. Military mission control visual language consistent with the cosmological theme.

---

## 15. What Was Fixed and Built — All Sessions

### Prior Sessions (2026-03-19) — Summary

Fixes 1-10 applied in earlier sessions:
1. Vercel API routes rewritten to Node.js handler style (save/load/world-settings were crashing with 500 errors)
2. Admin time-scale requests now include the Clerk JWT (were silently returning 401)
3. Mouse cursor released when any panel opens
4. Dead event listeners removed from SceneRoot
5. Click-to-play overlay added (game was unenterable without it)
6. Camera distance increased from 6m to 8m
7. Admin WebSocket variable correctly assigned
8. Rapier3D physics fully integrated — planet trimesh collider, player capsule, character controller, gravity toward planet center
9. Terrain triangle winding fixed — player no longer falls through the floor
10. DevUser ghost copy removed — player's own data no longer stored as a remote entity

---

### Session: Simulation Grid Phase 1 — 2026-03-20

Built the first real chemistry simulation: MaterialRegistry, GridCoords, Arrhenius pure modules, per-material thermal worker, chemistry worker (10 Hz, IGNITE/PLACE_MATERIAL/TICK messages), LocalSimManager, FireRenderer (glowing spheres + point lights), HUD ambient temperature display. 18 Vitest unit tests added and passing.

---

### Session: Status Website — 2026-03-20

Built the complete Mission Control dashboard at /status/: useStatusSocket hook, EpochBar, SatelliteMap (procedural terrain + live entity dots), ServerStats, PlayerRoster, PlayerDetail. Second Vite entry point added. Full documentation in Section 14.

---

### Session: M0 Universe Visible — 2026-03-21 (before this report)

- Ambient fires: 6 wood clusters pre-ignite at world initialization. Fire is visible within seconds of spawning without any player action.
- Day/night cycle: 20-minute real-time full cycle. DayNightCycle.tsx rotates the sun around the planet Y-axis. Rayleigh scatter at dawn/dusk. Ambient light follows cosine curve.
- Biome temperatures: sim grid cells initialize to biome-correct temperatures. Mountain peaks -5C. Desert lowlands 35C. Sea level temperate 15C. HUD temperature is now meaningful and non-static.
- Organisms: 10 creatures spawned from genome encoder at world start. Wander AI active. CreatureRenderer draws them with subsurface scattering.
- SimGrid Visualizer: Tab-toggle overlay using THREE.DataTexture fed by a GLSL shader. Black-body color ramp from blue (cold) through yellow (warm) to red (hot).
- Fire photorealism: flickering lights (sin wave + noise), color gradient from deep red through orange to white-hot, smoke plume above flame, billboard sprites.
- Tree wind sway: vertex shader wind animation.

---

### Session: M1-M4 Survival Slices — 2026-03-21 (before this report)

All 7 survival slices verified end-to-end:
- Slice 1: Gather rock/wood to inventory (live hotbar, 200ms polling)
- Slice 2: Craft stone tool, equip, chop tree (3-hit system, health bars)
- Slice 3: Flint + wood = fire via real Arrhenius chemistry
- Slice 4: Cook food over fire (sim thermodynamics), eat, hunger restores
- Slice 5: Wound = logistic bacterial growth, herb treatment heals
- Slice 6: Build shelter, sleep, stamina restores
- Slice 7: Smelt copper (Cu2S + C + heat = Cu + SO2), craft copper tools

### Session: M8 Weather — 2026-03-21

- Track 1: WeatherRenderer built and active (rain/snow/storm/wind/clouds, Markov-chain state machine). Fire extinguishing during rain is in progress.
- Track 2: Steel age (Fe + C carburization, quenching mechanic, steel tools and armor) — planned.

### Session: M9 Rivers + Animals + Performance — 2026-03-21

**Track 1 — Rivers**
- RiverSystem.ts: flow-field erosion algorithm generates 10 rivers per world seed, carving valleys 5-15m deep into the terrain heightmap
- Valley geometry is real 3D terrain modification — visible depressions in the planet surface
- RiverRenderer.tsx: ribbon mesh (triangle strip following the channel) with a custom Fresnel water shader that mimics real water surface optics
- KCC current push: Rapier character controller receives a lateral impulse when the player enters river water, physically pushing them downstream
- Drinkable freshwater: pressing F near river water restores the hydration vital (replaces random puddles as the primary water source)
- Clay nodes: clay resource spawns deterministically on riverbanks rather than scattered across the terrain
- RiverHUD: droplet icon appears in the HUD when the player is within drinking range of a river

**Track 2 — Animals**
- AnimalAISystem.ts: three species with distinct behavior profiles
  - Deer: flock (separation/cohesion/alignment), flee from players and wolves
  - Wolves: travel in packs, coordinate hunts (driver/flanker roles), attack players in range
  - Boars: solitary/small groups, charge behavior when threatened
- AnimalRenderer.tsx: InstancedMesh for each species (one GPU draw call per species), procedural geometry (no external model files), 3-tier LOD (full / reduced / billboard)
- Ecosystem population caps per species — no runaway spawning
- Hunting loot: raw_meat, hide, bone, wolf_pelt, boar_tusk
- New crafting recipes: leather (process hide at fire), leather armor (5 leather + 2 bone needle), bone needle (1 bone), tusk knife (boar_tusk + bone)

**Track 3 — Performance**
- Worker tick rates standardized: fluid/chemistry at 20 Hz, thermal at 10 Hz
- Creature LOD: 3 tiers selected by camera distance — reduces GPU load when many animals are visible
- WebSocket BATCH_UPDATE: non-critical state deltas batched into one message instead of individual sends
- NodeHealthBars pool: 32 pre-allocated health bar objects, reused per-interaction to eliminate garbage collection pauses
- Terrain shader warmup: gl.compile() called at scene initialization so the terrain shader is compiled before the first frame it is needed

---

### Session: M10 Seasons + Sailing + Trade Economy — 2026-03-21

**Track A — Seasons**
- SeasonSystem.js: 40-minute year, 4 seasons of equal length (10 minutes each), season name and day-of-year computed from world sim_time
- SeasonalTerrainPass.tsx: per-season terrain overlays — autumn amber, winter snow, spring blossoms, summer baseline
- Winter metabolic penalty: hunger drain rate multiplied by 1.20 during winter season
- SeasonWidget added to HUD: displays current season name and day-of-year counter
- SEASON_CHANGED WebSocket message broadcast to all clients on transition

**Track B — Sailing and Fishing**
- SailingSystem.ts: wind-driven raft (downwind only) and sailing boat (full tacking, 45-degree no-go zone), physics reads wind state from WeatherRenderer
- SailingRenderer.tsx: procedural meshes for both craft — no external 3D model files required
- Fishing system: Fishing Rod item (ITEM 62), cast-and-wait mechanic, 5-15 second random bite timer, reaction-window catch prompt
- Compass (ITEM 61): displays magnetic bearing in HUD overlay while held
- New items: RAFT (ITEM 59), SAILING_BOAT (ITEM 60), COMPASS (ITEM 61), FISHING_ROD (ITEM 62)
- New recipes 81-87: raft, sailing boat, compass, fishing rod, cooked fish, salt, smoked fish

**Track C — Trade Economy**
- TradeEconomy.js: settlement_stockpiles table in Neon DB, supply/demand pricing formula (0.6x floor to 2.5x ceiling relative to base price)
- Five settlement specializations defined: Ashford (wood/leather), Ironhaven (metals), Saltmere (fish/salt), Thornwall (stone/ore), Ridgepost (food/grain)
- NPC caravans: one caravan spawns every 5 real-time minutes, walks from one settlement to another, transfers goods, updates stockpile rows in DB
- ShopHUD.tsx: buy tab and sell tab, live prices fetched from stockpile state, copper coin transactions
- New materials: COPPER_COIN (MAT 59), FISH (MAT 60), SALT (MAT 61), GRAIN (MAT 62)
- New WebSocket messages: SHOP_BUY, SHOP_SELL, STOCKPILE_UPDATE, CARAVAN_SPAWNED
- Next available IDs at M10 completion: MAT 63, ITEM 63, Recipe 88

### Session: M11 Gunpowder + Castles + Astronomy + Diplomacy — 2026-03-21

**Gunpowder Chemistry**
- Added KNO3, carbon, and sulfur as reagents in the Arrhenius chemistry grid
- Saltpeter (MAT 63) forms procedurally in nitrogen-rich soil near animal waste deposits and cave floor tiles
- Sulfur (MAT 64) appears in volcanic terrain zones; sourcing is geographically locked to volcanic biomes
- KNO3 + C + S reaction produces Rocket Fuel / gunpowder (MAT 65) at threshold temperature — same real reaction kinetics as wood combustion
- Musket (ITEM 63): iron barrel sub-component + powder charge; first chemical propulsion firearm in the game
- Recipe IDs 88-90 cover the gunpowder chain

**Castle Structures**
- Three new construction blocks: wall segment, tower (corner and inline), gate (player-operable)
- CastleRenderer.tsx: unified renderer for all three block types; shares material atlas
- Siege mechanics: gates and walls have structural health points; attackers can batter them down
- City walls unlock for NPC settlements at civLevel 4; player-constructed castles unlock at civLevel 5

**Astronomy**
- Star particle system updated with real IAU constellation position data (replaces generic 5,000-point star field)
- Glass Ingot (MAT 66): kiln recipe — sand + heat
- Telescope (ITEM 64): 4 iron ingot + 2 glass ingot; requires civLevel 3
- Equipped telescope at night reveals constellation labels in a HUD overlay
- Moon phase is tracked against world clock; next eclipse time is computed from orbital mechanics and shown in astronomy HUD

**Diplomacy**
- Mayor NPC appointment: each settlement promotes the NPC with the highest accumulated trust score as mayor
- Diplomatic envoys: named NPC walks visibly from one settlement to another when diplomatic contact is initiated
- War and peace declarations: settlements can enter formal war or peace state; player's criminal alignment shifts based on which faction they attack or assist
- New WebSocket message: DIPLOMACY_STATE_CHANGED (settlementA, settlementB, state: war|peace|neutral)

---

### Session: M12 Space Age — 2026-03-21

**Rocket System**
- RocketSystem.ts: countdown (T-10) → ignition → ascent state machine
- On ascent: ROCKET_LAUNCHED WebSocket broadcast sent to all clients; screen shake effect fires
- Rocket position tracked server-side during ascent phase

**Rocket VFX**
- RocketVFXRenderer.tsx: three-layer exhaust cone — white-hot inner core, orange-amber middle, dark soot outer dispersal; 180 particles total
- ShaderMaterial heat shimmer distorts geometry above the exhaust plume
- Scorch disc spawns on terrain at launch pad after liftoff

**Radio System**
- RadioSystem.ts: persistent broadcast queue and tower registry maintained server-side
- PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST WebSocket message sent when a player activates a tower
- RadioTowerVFXRenderer.tsx: teal EM pulse rings using additive Three.js blending; 1 ring per 2 seconds per active tower

**Electric Lighting**
- ElectricLightPass.tsx: activates at civLevel 6 on any settlement
- 40 Three.js PointLights per settlement at 2700K warm white (#FFBA74), night-only, with filament flicker

**civLevel 6 Gate and L7 Teaser**
- CIVILIZATION_L6 WebSocket broadcast on civLevel 6 threshold
- Unlocks four tech branches: electronics, rocketry, aerospace, nuclear physics
- VelarSignalView.tsx: Morse-blink signal panel, activates 30 seconds after first ROCKET_LAUNCHED in session; teaser text for L7 content

**New material and item IDs (M11-M12)**
- MAT 63: Saltpeter, MAT 64: Sulfur, MAT 65: Rocket Fuel, MAT 66: Circuit Board, MAT 67: Nuclear Fuel
- ITEM 63: Musket, ITEM 64: Telescope, ITEM 65: Artillery Shell
- Recipes 88-99: full M11-M12 crafting chains
- Next available IDs: MAT 68, ITEM 66, Recipe 100

## 16. Known Issues and Gaps

### Active Bugs

| ID | Issue | Severity | Impact |
|---|---|---|---|
| B-01 | admin.ts uses Web API style (Request/Response) and returns 500 errors in production | Medium | Admin player list fails to load. Admin panel opens but shows no players. |
| B-02 | No toast notification when auto-save fails | Low | Players have no feedback if save silently fails |
| B-03 | @clerk/clerk-react and @clerk/react both listed at different versions | Low | May cause import conflicts or bundle bloat |
| B-04 | Simulation grid state (fire, heat) is not persisted | Low | Active fires are lost on disconnect or page refresh |
| B-05 | Status site canvas is fixed at 800x600 and stretches to fill container | Low | Click detection may be slightly imprecise at extreme aspect ratios |
| B-06 | Fire extinguishing during rain not yet wired to chemistry grid | Medium | WeatherRenderer exists but rain does not yet affect in-world fire state |

### Missing Gameplay Features

| Feature | Why It Matters |
|---|---|
| Steel age (M8 Track 2) | Required for steel tools and armor — the next progression tier after iron |
| Fire spread to adjacent cells | Fire burns only in the ignited cell, not to neighboring combustible cells |
| Tech Tree panel content | Panel opens but shows nothing |
| Evolution panel content | Panel opens but shows nothing |
| Journal panel content | Panel opens but shows nothing |
| Settings panel content | Panel opens but shows nothing |
| Mobile and touch support | Game requires mouse and keyboard — not playable on phones or tablets |
| Save-failed notification | Error path in saveGame() has no user-facing feedback |
| Level-of-detail system for terrain and buildings | Animals have LOD; terrain and placed structures do not |
| Wolf pelt recipes | Wolf pelts are collectible but have no crafting recipe yet |
| Sailing boat multiplayer sync | Watercraft position is not yet broadcast over WebSocket — other players cannot see a boat moving |
| Fishing rod animation | No cast animation — the rod appears static while waiting for a bite |
| Settlement stockpile migration | settlement_stockpiles table requires manual creation; init-db does not yet create it automatically |
| ElectricLightPass scale | 40 PointLights per civLevel 6 settlement — performance impact at multiple simultaneous civLevel 6 settlements is untested (M12) |
| VelarSignalView content | L7 teaser signal panel is a Morse-blink placeholder — no gameplay content behind it yet (M12) |
| Artillery Shell (ITEM 65) | ID assigned but no delivery system (cannon, siege engine) built yet — item is currently an inert inventory collectible (M12) |
| Nuclear Fuel (MAT 67) | ID assigned; nuclear reactor and fuel rod crafting exists in the recipe table but the building and operation system is not yet implemented (M12) |

---

## 17. Recommendations

### Critical (fix immediately)

1. **Fix admin.ts API handler style** — Rewrite to Node.js req/res style matching the other three routes. The admin player list is a production failure today.
2. **Add WebSocket authentication for ADMIN_SET_TIME** — Any player can currently change the world clock. Add a server-side admin check using the player's registered userId.

### High Priority

3. **Wire fire extinguishing to chemistry grid** — WeatherRenderer sets isRain but the chemistry worker does not yet extinguish cells. This is M8 Track 1's remaining work.
4. **Complete steel age chain** — Fe + C carburization and quenching mechanic to unlock the next progression tier.
5. **Add save-failure toast notification** — Players need feedback when auto-save fails.

### Medium Priority

6. **Add settlement_stockpiles to init-db** — The M10 trade economy requires this table. It is not yet created automatically by /api/init-db, meaning a fresh deployment will fail to load shop prices until the table is added manually.
7. **Sync watercraft position over WebSocket** — Sailing boats are not currently broadcast to other players. This makes multiplayer sailing invisible to other clients.
8. **Remove duplicate @clerk/clerk-react dependency** — Clean up package.json to remove version 5; use only @clerk/react version 6.
9. **Migrate discoveries column to JSONB** — Change the TEXT column to a native Postgres JSONB column so discoveries can be queried and indexed.
10. **Add fire spread logic** — Check adjacent cells for combustible materials each tick and ignite if temperature exceeds neighbor's ignition threshold.
11. **Populate Tech Tree panel** — Even a basic read-only view of the tech tree would give players meaningful goals.
12. **Add test coverage for M10-M12 systems** — SeasonSystem, SailingSystem, TradeEconomy, RocketSystem, and RadioSystem have no unit tests. Season timing math, price formula, and rocket state machine transitions are strong candidates for deterministic unit tests.
13. **Benchmark ElectricLightPass with multiple civLevel 6 settlements** — 40 PointLights is within Three.js's comfort zone for one settlement, but the total light count will multiply as the world advances. Profile before the next milestone adds more settlements.
14. **Implement artillery shell delivery system** — ITEM 65 (Artillery Shell) has an ID and recipe but no cannon or siege engine to fire it. The hunting loop for this item is incomplete.
15. **Implement nuclear reactor building** — MAT 67 (Nuclear Fuel) and the associated recipe exist, but the nuclear power building and its operation cycle are not yet built. Complete the energy chain started in M12.

### Low Priority / Long-term

13. **Add level-of-detail for terrain and buildings** — Animals have three LOD tiers; terrain and placed structures render at full detail at all distances.
14. **Persist simulation grid state** — Save fire and heat state to the database so it survives disconnection.
15. **Protect or remove /api/init-db** — Move behind admin auth or delete after initial setup.
16. **Add test coverage for survival systems** — SurvivalSystems.ts, DeathSystem.ts, SettlementStore, and OutlawStore are untested.
17. **Add fishing rod cast animation** — The rod is visually static during the wait period; a cast arc and line would improve readability.
18. **Add wolf pelt crafting recipe** — Wolf pelts are collected but unused. A pelt cloak or warm clothing item would complete the hunting loop.

## 18. Summary Scorecard

| Area | Rating | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Core Survival Loop | Excellent | All 7 slices verified end-to-end; freshwater now from rivers |
| Chemistry and Physics | Excellent | Real Arrhenius combustion, reduction chemistry, gunpowder Arrhenius reaction (M11), Rapier3D physics, river current push |
| Rivers | Excellent | Flow-field erosion, real valley geometry, Fresnel water, KCC push, drinkable freshwater (M9) |
| Seasons | Excellent | 40-min year, 4 seasons, terrain overlays, winter metabolic penalty, HUD widget (M10) |
| Sailing and Fishing | Good | Wind-driven raft and sailing boat with tacking, fishing rod, compass navigation (M10) — watercraft not yet multiplayer-synced |
| Trade Economy | Good | Live supply/demand, settlement specializations, NPC caravans, ShopHUD, copper coins (M10) — stockpile table migration is manual |
| Animal AI | Good | Three species with distinct behavioral profiles; InstancedMesh rendering; ecosystem caps (M9) |
| Multiplayer | Good | Server-authoritative, 10 Hz sync, BATCH_UPDATE, ROCKET_LAUNCHED and PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST added (M12) — sailing still not synced |
| NPC Civilization | Good | 5 settlements, mayor NPCs, diplomatic envoys, war/peace declarations (M11), live economy (M10) |
| PvP and Outlaw System | Good | murder_count in DB, bounties, redemption quests, criminal alignment now affected by diplomacy (M11) |
| Visual Quality | Excellent | Photorealism pass, day/night, weather, Fresnel rivers, seasonal overlays, real star constellations, rocket VFX 3-layer exhaust, electric settlement lights (M12) |
| Performance | Good | Workers offloaded, instanced rendering, LOD on animals, NodeHealthBar pool, shader warmup; ElectricLightPass 40 PointLights per settlement needs benchmarking at scale (M12) |
| Security | Needs Improvement | Admin WebSocket unauthenticated, admin.ts broken in production |
| Code Quality | Good | Clean structure, well-commented, test coverage only for sim modules — M10-M12 systems untested |
| Database and Data | Good | NPC memory, criminal records, world settings, settlement stockpiles all persisted |
| Accessibility | Needs Improvement | Screen reader labels present but no keyboard-only play mode |
| Panel Completeness | Needs Improvement | 4 of 8 sidebar panels are empty placeholders; Tech Tree unlocked by civLevel 6 gate but panel still placeholder |
| Companion Site | Good | Live at https://universe-companion.vercel.app (M9) |
| Gunpowder and Firearms | Good | Real KNO3+C+S Arrhenius reaction, saltpeter and sulfur sourcing, musket as first chemical firearm (M11) |
| Castle and Fortification | Good | Multi-block wall/tower/gate, CastleRenderer, siege mechanics, civLevel 4-5 unlock (M11) |
| Astronomy | Good | Real constellation positions, telescope item, moon phase tracking, eclipse timing, night-sky HUD (M11) |
| Diplomacy | Good | Mayor NPCs, diplomatic envoys, war/peace declarations affecting criminal alignment (M11) |
| Space Age / Rocketry | Good | Countdown/ignition/ascent state machine, 3-layer exhaust VFX, screen shake, ROCKET_LAUNCHED broadcast (M12) |
| Radio | Good | Broadcast queue, tower registry, teal EM pulse rings, PLAYER_RADIO_BROADCAST (M12) |
| Electric Lighting | Good | 40 tungsten PointLights per civLevel 6 settlement, night-only, natural flicker (M12) — scale performance unverified |
| M8 Remaining | In Progress | Fire extinguishing during rain and steel age still pending |
| **Overall Health** | **Good** | **The game now spans Stone Age through Space Age. M11 and M12 together deliver gunpowder chemistry, real constellation astronomy, castle fortifications, diplomatic NPCs, rocket launch VFX, radio broadcasting, and electric settlement lighting. Primary gaps: watercraft multiplayer sync, ElectricLightPass benchmarking at scale, and several M12 items (artillery shell, nuclear reactor) that have IDs and recipes but no operational system yet.** |
